unanchored_metrics.source: HRSA Data Warehouse — Health Professional Shortage Areas (Dental HPSA)
unanchored_metrics.reason: HRSA does not publish a national-mean HPSA score; the metric is a definitional shortage-area severity index (0–25) where calibration to a published mean is not meaningful.

validation_studies.title: Maternal access composite vs. birth outcomes
validation_studies.hypothesis: Counties with worse maternal access (higher composite) should show higher infant mortality and low birth weight rates.
validation_studies.headline: r=0.28 vs IM, r=0.09 vs LBW

methodology.pivot_rationale: CDC WONDER API blocks county-level grouping for vital statistics; >90% of US counties have suppressed maternal mortality at the county level. Pivoted to validating maternalAccess against perinatal outcomes (infant mortality, low birth weight) which have strong county coverage in CHR&R 2025.
methodology.composite_definition: maternalAccessComposite = mean(maternityCareDesert/3, obProvidersDeficit, obUnitClosure, clamp(distance/30, 0, 1)). Range 0-1.
methodology.outcomes_independent: Both outcomes (LBW, infant mortality) are NOT inputs to the maternalAccess composite. They ARE inputs to the separate `perinatal` HEG component but that component is computed independently from maternalAccess.

low_birth_weight.outcome: Low Birth Weight (% of live births)

validation_studies.title: Health Equity Gap (HEG) vs. premature death
validation_studies.hypothesis: Counties with higher HEG should have higher premature death rates (years of potential life lost).
validation_studies.headline: r≈0.677 vs YPLL

premature_death.source: CHR&R 2025 — Premature Death (YPLL75 per 100,000) — NCHS underlying source

preventable_stays.source: CHR&R 2025 — Preventable Hospital Stays (per 100,000 Medicare enrollees) — CMS source

validation_studies.title: Behavioral health burden vs. drug overdose + suicide mortality
validation_studies.hypothesis: Counties with higher BH burden (depression, FMD, lack of emotional support, loneliness) should show higher drug overdose and suicide mortality.
validation_studies.headline: r=0.401 vs drug overdose, r=0.227 vs suicide

methodology.hypothesis: If the behavioral health (BH) burden of a county is real, counties with higher BH burden should have higher rates of behavioral-health mortality outcomes (drug overdose, suicide).
methodology.bh_burden_index: mean(depression/30, frequent_mental_distress/22, lack_emotional_support/25, loneliness/35), each clamped 0-1.
methodology.circularity_check: drug_overdose and suicide are NOT inputs to bh_burden_index. They ARE inputs to HEG's behavHealthGap component, but bh_burden_index is constructed independently to keep the test clean.

validation_studies.title: Maternal access composite — ceiling diagnosis
validation_studies.hypothesis: Question: is the modest r=0.28 vs infant mortality a formula artifact, or are the underlying inputs themselves signal-limited?
validation_studies.headline: optimal multiple R = 0.319 vs IM, 0.220 vs LBW — reweighting cannot meaningfully exceed the current composite’s r=0.28

interpretation.compares_components: Each predictor variant correlated against IM and LBW alone
interpretation.goal: Identify whether the maternal access signal is in any single component, and whether reweighting the composite would meaningfully improve r vs perinatal outcomes
